description_long: This fire hydrant features a cylindrical body with a domed top and base, both adorned with vertical metallic ridges and bolts. The main body is a weathered, dark brown metal, giving it a rustic, aged appearance, while the metallic accents are a lighter, silvery color. Two large hose connection outlets protrude from either side, each capped with a hexagonal nut, and a third, smaller outlet is visible on the front, also capped. The top of the hydrant has a central operating nut, and the base is reinforced with a thick, ribbed collar. One side of the hydrant displays a faint, stylized emblem or marking, possibly a manufacturer's logo. The overall construction suggests durability and functionality, typical of hydrants found in urban environments.
description: A weathered, brown metal fire hydrant with silver accents, three capped hose outlets, and a domed top. It features bolts, ridges, and a faint emblem on one side.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central capped outlet and symmetrical side outlets with prominent bolts and metallic accents.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting one capped outlet and the domed top, with the side nut and bolts clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the faint emblem or marking on the main body, with both side outlets visible.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the other capped outlet and the domed top, mirroring view 1.
